title: Icacorea aurantiaca
intro: Icacorea aurantiaca, also known as the yellow icacorea, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Icacorea aurantiaca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Icacorea aurantiaca is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Icacorea aurantiaca belongs to the family Icacinaceae. The genus Icacorea is a part of the order Icacinales. This plant's classification is further categorized under the class Magnoliopsida. The order Icacinales is a part of the clade Pentapetalae. The plant's classification is an example of the complex relationships within the plant kingdom.
